response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and systems software engineer with strong breadth across web, mobile, and embedded domains, but his profile has virtually no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware design, deep neural network workload optimization, and SIMD/MIMD architecture — none of which appear in his resume. His current AI-adjacent work at Google involves agent infrastructure and developer tooling at the software layer, not hardware or silicon-level performance analysis. With only a BS and no hardware architecture background, he does not meet the experience threshold for a principal-level hardware-software co-design role.
strengths: Long tenure across high-caliber companies (Google, Meta) demonstrating consistent career progression and technical credibility
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/sec) shows performance-aware engineering instincts
Breadth across embedded systems, robotics, and infrastructure tooling suggests adaptability beyond pure web development
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or hardware architecture experience of any kind — the core technical requirement of this role
No deep learning training or inference optimization background; AI exposure is limited to agent tooling at the application layer
Principal-level role requires 8+ years in a directly relevant field; his 15+ years are almost entirely in web and application software, not hardware or systems architecture
